title: Methanol-Chloroform-Water Precipitation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1mu4j","text":"Methanol-chloroform-water (MCW) precipitation is a rapid method for removing interferring species from protein samples prior to LC-MS analysis. MCW precipitation can effectively remove both ionic and non-ionic detergents and salts. Precipitating less than 1 µg protein is challenging yet possible with careful pipetting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1p9og","text":"Halogenated solvent waste should be disposed properly as it poses environmental hazards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Dilute protein sample to 150 µL with Optima-grade water.
Wash pellet with methanol. (This step can be repeated if a high level of contaminating species is anticipated)
Add 600 µL Optima-grade methanol and mix well.
Add 150 µL HPLC-grade chloroform and mix well.
Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes and discard top layer while being careful to not disturb interface between layers. Use a gel-loading tip to remove last bit.
Add 600 µL and homogenize the solvent, being careful not to break up the precipitated protein.
Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, and discard supernatant, being careful not to disturb pellet.
Add 600 µL methanol and mix by inverting tube.
Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, and discard supernatant, being careful not to disturb pellet.
Evaporate excess methanol by laying tube on its side for 5 min at room temperature.
Redissolve precipitated protein in 30 µL Buffer A. Incubate 10 min on ice. If protein is not completely dissolved, sample can be briefly sonicated with a bath sonicator.
Centrifuge 20000 x g, 4 °C, and transfer supernatant to LC sample vial.
creator: Kelleher KRG KRG Research Group
